title: Syzygium subalatum
intro: Syzygium subalatum, also known as the Mount Solitary Syzygium, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae, native to the Blue Mountains of New South Wales in Australia. The leaves and fruits of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in relieving symptoms of fever, pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium subalatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Syzygium subalatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Syzygium and the order Myrtales. The plant is native to New Caledonia, a region in the Pacific Ocean. Syzygium subalatum is a member of the Myrtaceae family, which includes other well-known plants such as the eucalyptus and the guava. The classification of Syzygium subalatum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Myrtaceae family.

properties: Syzygium subalatum has been found to have various therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine.
